description: A comprehensive customer analytics pipeline that processes raw transactional, web engagement, shipping, and promotional data to create a unified customer 360-degree view. The pipeline calculates customer lifetime value, RFM segmentation, tier assignments, cross-sell recommendations, and engagement metrics for business intelligence and marketing analytics.

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: Type of web event (page_view, add_to_cart, search, wishlist)

columns.name: time_on_page_seconds
columns.description: Time spent on page in seconds

columns.name: lifetime_revenue
columns.description: Total revenue from customer in USD

columns.name: online_pct
columns.description: Percentage of orders that were online

columns.name: payment_methods_used
columns.description: Number of distinct payment methods used

columns.name: avg_time_on_page
columns.description: Average time on page in seconds

components.name: active_customers
components.description: Filters customers to active and trial status only, normalizes names (UPPER/TRIM), calculates age and days since signup. Excludes customers without email or who signed up before 2018.
components.sql_text: WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'trial') AND c.email IS NOT NULL AND c.signup_date >= '2018-01-01'

components.name: category_hierarchy
components.description: Builds complete category hierarchy paths using recursive CTE. Creates full path strings like 'Electronics > Computers > Laptops' and tracks depth level up to 5 levels deep.
components.sql_text: RECURSIVE CTE: CAST(ch.full_path + ' > ' + child.category_name AS VARCHAR(500)) WHERE ch.depth < 5

components.name: enriched_products
components.description: Enriches products with category hierarchy information and calculates profit margins. Categorizes products as High/Medium/Low margin based on margin percentage thresholds (>50%, >25%).
components.sql_text: CASE WHEN (p.unit_price - p.cost_price) / NULLIF(p.unit_price, 0) > 0.5 THEN 'High Margin' WHEN > 0.25 THEN 'Medium Margin' ELSE 'Low Margin' END

components.name: exchange_rates_latest
components.description: Gets the most recent exchange rate for each currency pair using ROW_NUMBER window function partitioned by currency pair and ordered by date descending.
components.sql_text: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY from_currency, to_currency ORDER BY rate_date DESC) WHERE rn = 1

components.name: transactions_normalized
components.description: Converts all transaction amounts to USD using latest exchange rates. Calculates gross and net amounts, categorizes channels into Online/Offline groups. Filters out cancelled and fraudulent transactions from 2018 onwards.
components.sql_text: t.unit_price_local * t.quantity * COALESCE(er.rate, 1.0) AS gross_amount_usd, CASE WHEN t.channel IN ('web','mobile') THEN 'Online' WHEN IN ('store','phone') THEN 'Offline' ELSE 'Other' END

components.name: returns_summary
components.description: Aggregates return metrics per customer-product combination including return count, total refund amount, last return date, and concatenated return reasons.
components.sql_text: COUNT(*) AS return_count, SUM(r.refund_amount) AS total_refund, STRING_AGG(r.return_reason, '; ') AS return_reasons GROUP BY customer_id, product_id

components.name: customer_transactions
components.description: Aggregates comprehensive transaction metrics per customer including order counts, revenue totals, average order value, date ranges, channel splits, and median order value using PERCENTILE_CONT.
components.sql_text: COUNT(DISTINCT tn.transaction_id) AS total_orders, SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) AS lifetime_revenue, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY tn.net_amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id) AS median_order_value

components.name: customer_returns
components.description: Aggregates return metrics per customer including total returns, refund amounts, last return date, and count of distinct products returned.
components.sql_text: SUM(rs.return_count) AS total_returns, SUM(rs.total_refund) AS total_refund_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT rs.product_id) AS products_returned GROUP BY customer_id

components.name: customer_web_activity
components.description: Aggregates web engagement metrics per customer including event counts by type, session metrics, time on page, and calculates bounce rate as sessions with only 1 event divided by total sessions.
components.sql_text: SUM(CASE WHEN we.event_type = 'page_view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS page_views, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN we.session_events = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT we.session_id), 0) AS bounce_rate

components.name: top_products_per_customer
components.description: Ranks products by revenue per customer using ROW_NUMBER window function. Calculates total revenue and quantity per customer-product combination to identify top performing products.
components.sql_text: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id ORDER BY SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) DESC) AS product_rank

components.name: top_categories_per_customer
components.description: Ranks product categories by spend per customer using ROW_NUMBER window function. Aggregates spend and order count per customer-category combination.
components.sql_text: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id ORDER BY SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) DESC) AS category_rank

components.name: promotion_usage
components.description: Identifies which promotions each customer used by joining transactions with promotions based on date ranges and optional product matching. Calculates usage frequency and total discount savings.
components.sql_text: JOIN ON tn.order_date BETWEEN p.start_date AND p.end_date AND (p.product_id IS NULL OR p.product_id = tn.product_id) WHERE tn.discount_amount > 0

components.name: customer_promo_summary
components.description: Summarizes promotion usage per customer including count of distinct promotions used, total savings, last promotion type, and concatenated promotion names.
components.sql_text: COUNT(DISTINCT pu.promo_id) AS promotions_used, SUM(pu.total_discount_usd) AS total_promo_savings, STRING_AGG(DISTINCT pu.promo_name, ', ') AS promo_names

components.name: shipping_metrics
components.description: Calculates shipping performance metrics per customer including average delivery time, fastest/slowest delivery, late delivery count, total shipping costs, and most frequently used shipping method via correlated subquery.
components.sql_text: AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, s.ship_date, s.delivery_date)) AS avg_delivery_days, (SELECT TOP 1 s2.shipping_method FROM raw.shipping s2 WHERE s2.customer_id = s.customer_id GROUP BY s2.shipping_method ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS preferred_shipping_method

components.name: monthly_spend
components.description: Creates monthly time series of customer spend by grouping transactions by customer and month using DATEFROMPARTS to create month start dates.
components.sql_text: DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(tn.order_date), MONTH(tn.order_date), 1) AS month_start, SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) AS monthly_revenue GROUP BY customer_id, month_start

components.name: spend_trend
components.description: Calculates spend trends using window functions including LAG/LEAD for previous/next month comparison, rolling 3-month average, rolling 6-month total, and NTILE for spend quartiles.
components.sql_text: LAG(ms.monthly_revenue, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY ms.customer_id ORDER BY ms.month_start) AS prev_month_revenue, AVG(ms.monthly_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY ms.customer_id ORDER BY ms.month_start ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_3m_avg

components.name: latest_spend_trend
components.description: Extracts the most recent month's trend metrics per customer and calculates month-over-month growth percentage using the previous month's revenue.
components.sql_text: CASE WHEN st.prev_month_revenue IS NULL OR = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (st.monthly_revenue - st.prev_month_revenue) / st.prev_month_revenue * 100.0 END AS mom_growth_pct WHERE month_start = MAX(month_start)

components.name: rfm_scores
components.description: Calculates RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) scores using NTILE window functions to create 5-point scales for customer segmentation analysis.
components.sql_text: NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY ct.days_since_last_order ASC) AS recency_score, NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY ct.total_orders DESC) AS frequency_score, NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY ct.lifetime_revenue DESC) AS monetary_score

components.name: rfm_segment
components.description: Maps RFM scores to business-meaningful segment names using complex CASE logic: Champion (high scores across all), Loyal (medium-high), New Customer (high recency, low frequency), At Risk (low recency, high frequency/monetary), Lost (low across all), Big Spender (high monetary), Regular (default).
components.sql_text: CASE WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 4 AND rfm.frequency_score >= 4 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 4 THEN 'Champion' WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 3 AND rfm.frequency_score >= 3 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 3 THEN 'Loyal' WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 4 AND rfm.frequency_score <= 2 THEN 'New Customer' WHEN rfm.recency_score <= 2 AND rfm.frequency_score >= 3 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 3 THEN 'At Risk' WHEN rfm.recency_score <= 2 AND rfm.frequency_score <= 2 AND rfm.monetary_score <= 2 THEN 'Lost' WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 3 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 4 THEN 'Big Spender' ELSE 'Regular' END

components.name: customer_tier_calc
components.description: Assigns customer tiers based on lifetime revenue and order count thresholds: Platinum (≥$50K & ≥50 orders), Gold (≥$20K & ≥20), Silver (≥$5K & ≥5), Bronze (all others). Also calculates revenue rank and percentile, and engagement status based on days since last order.
components.sql_text: CASE WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 50000 AND ct.total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 20000 AND ct.total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 5000 AND ct.total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze' END AS customer_tier, CASE WHEN ct.days_since_last_order <= 30 THEN 'Active' WHEN <= 90 THEN 'Warm' WHEN <= 180 THEN 'Cooling' ELSE 'Dormant' END AS engagement_status

components.name: cross_sell_candidates
components.description: Identifies cross-sell opportunities by finding frequently co-purchased categories that a customer hasn't tried yet. Uses complex joins to find category pairs with ≥10 co-purchasing customers, then filters out categories the customer has already purchased.
components.sql_text: Complex multi-table join to find co-purchased categories with COUNT(DISTINCT tn1.customer_id) >= 10, filtered by NOT EXISTS for categories customer hasn't purchased

components.name: cross_sell_agg
components.description: Aggregates cross-sell recommendations per customer, keeping top 5 recommendations by co-purchase frequency and concatenating category names into a comma-separated list.
components.sql_text: STRING_AGG(csc.co_category, ', ') AS recommended_categories WHERE ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY co_purchase_count DESC) <= 5

components.name: predicted_ltv
components.description: Calculates predicted 3-year lifetime value using historical spend patterns. Formula: avg_order_value * monthly_order_frequency * 12 months * 3 years. Sets LTV to 0 for customers inactive >365 days. Segments customers as High/Medium/Low Value or Churned based on historical spend and activity.
components.sql_text: CASE WHEN ct.days_since_last_order > 365 THEN 0 ELSE ct.avg_order_value * (ct.total_orders / NULLIF(DATEDIFF(MONTH, ct.first_order_date, ct.last_order_date), 0)) * 12 * 3 END AS predicted_3yr_ltv

intermediate_steps.expression: c.customer_id

column_lineage.notes: Primary key flowing through from source customers table

intermediate_steps.expression: UPPER(TRIM(c.first_name)) AS first_name, UPPER(TRIM(c.last_name)) AS last_name

column_lineage.notes: Concatenates normalized first and last names with space separator

intermediate_steps.expression: DATEDIFF(YEAR, c.date_of_birth, GETDATE())

column_lineage.notes: Calculates customer age in years from birth date to current date

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN c.status = 'active' THEN 'Active' WHEN c.status = 'trial' THEN 'Trial' END

intermediate_steps.expression: CASE WHEN c.status = 'active' THEN 'Active' WHEN c.status = 'trial' THEN 'Trial' END

column_lineage.notes: Standardizes customer status values to proper case

intermediate_steps.expression: (t.unit_price_local * t.quantity - t.discount_amount) * COALESCE(er.rate, 1.0)
SUM(tn.net_amount_usd)

column_lineage.notes: Total net revenue from customer in USD after currency conversion and discounts

intermediate_steps.expression: (t.unit_price_local * t.quantity - t.discount_amount) * COALESCE(er.rate, 1.0)
AVG(tn.net_amount_usd)

column_lineage.notes: Average order value in USD across all customer transactions

column_lineage.transformation: PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY tn.net_amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id)

intermediate_steps.expression: (t.unit_price_local * t.quantity - t.discount_amount) * COALESCE(er.rate, 1.0)
PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY tn.net_amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id)

column_lineage.notes: Median order value using percentile function for more robust central tendency

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN COALESCE(ct.total_orders, 0) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE CAST(ct.online_orders AS FLOAT) / ct.total_orders * 100 END

intermediate_steps.expression: CASE WHEN t.channel IN ('web','mobile') THEN 'Online' WHEN t.channel IN ('store','phone') THEN 'Offline' ELSE 'Other' END
SUM(CASE WHEN tn.channel_group = 'Online' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)

column_lineage.notes: Percentage of orders placed through online channels (web/mobile)

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN COALESCE(ct.total_orders, 0) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE CAST(COALESCE(cr.total_returns, 0) AS FLOAT) / ct.total_orders * 100 END

intermediate_steps.expression: COUNT(*)
SUM(rs.return_count)

column_lineage.notes: Return rate as percentage of total orders

column_lineage.transformation: CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN we.session_events = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT we.session_id), 0)

intermediate_steps.expression: COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY we_inner.session_id) AS session_events
CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN we.session_events = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT we.session_id), 0)

column_lineage.notes: Bounce rate calculated as sessions with only 1 event divided by total sessions

column_lineage.transformation: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id ORDER BY SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) DESC) = 1

intermediate_steps.expression: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY tn.customer_id ORDER BY SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) DESC)

column_lineage.notes: Product name with highest revenue for this customer

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 50000 AND ct.total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 20000 AND ct.total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 5000 AND ct.total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze' END

intermediate_steps.expression: SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) AS lifetime_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT tn.transaction_id) AS total_orders

CASE WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 50000 AND ct.total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 20000 AND ct.total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' WHEN ct.lifetime_revenue >= 5000 AND ct.total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze' END

column_lineage.notes: Business tier assignment based on revenue and order count thresholds

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 4 AND rfm.frequency_score >= 4 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 4 THEN 'Champion' WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 3 AND rfm.frequency_score >= 3 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 3 THEN 'Loyal' WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 4 AND rfm.frequency_score <= 2 THEN 'New Customer' WHEN rfm.recency_score <= 2 AND rfm.frequency_score >= 3 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 3 THEN 'At Risk' WHEN rfm.recency_score <= 2 AND rfm.frequency_score <= 2 AND rfm.monetary_score <= 2 THEN 'Lost' WHEN rfm.recency_score >= 3 AND rfm.monetary_score >= 4 THEN 'Big Spender' ELSE 'Regular' END

intermediate_steps.expression: DATEDIFF(DAY, MAX(tn.order_date), GETDATE()) AS days_since_last_order, COUNT(DISTINCT tn.transaction_id) AS total_orders, SUM(tn.net_amount_usd) AS lifetime_revenue

NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY ct.days_since_last_order ASC) AS recency_score, NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY ct.total_orders DESC) AS frequency_score, NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY ct.lifetime_revenue DESC) AS monetary_score
Complex CASE WHEN logic mapping RFM scores to segment names

column_lineage.notes: RFM segmentation for customer behavior analysis and targeting

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN ct.days_since_last_order > 365 THEN 0 ELSE ct.avg_order_value * (ct.total_orders / NULLIF(DATEDIFF(MONTH, ct.first_order_date, ct.last_order_date), 0)) * 12 * 3 END

intermediate_steps.expression: AVG(tn.net_amount_usd) AS avg_order_value, COUNT(DISTINCT tn.transaction_id) AS total_orders, MIN(tn.order_date) AS first_order_date, MAX(tn.order_date) AS last_order_date
ct.avg_order_value * (ct.total_orders / NULLIF(DATEDIFF(MONTH, ct.first_order_date, ct.last_order_date), 0)) * 12 * 3

column_lineage.notes: Predicted 3-year lifetime value based on historical spend patterns and order frequency

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN rfm.rfm_segment_name IN ('Champion', 'Loyal', 'Big Spender') THEN 'Promoter' WHEN rfm.rfm_segment_name IN ('At Risk', 'Lost') THEN 'Detractor' ELSE 'Passive' END

intermediate_steps.expression: Complex RFM segment calculation

column_lineage.notes: Net Promoter Score proxy based on RFM segment classification
ETL processing timestamp for data lineage tracking
Model version identifier for tracking analytics logic changes

aggregate: return metrics per customer
